data.content: Two issues: implicit usings aren't enabled, and `Assert.ThrowsExactly` requires MSTest 3.7+. Let me fix both:

arguments.old_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.6.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
arguments.new_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.7.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.7.3" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
Two issues:
1. Missing `using System;` in the source file (or ImplicitUsings is not enabled)
2. `Assert.ThrowsExactly` requires MSTest v3.7+ (it was introduced in MSTest 3.7)

Let me check if ImplicitUsings is enabled and also upgrade MSTest to get ThrowsExactly.

    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.6.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
arguments.new_str:

    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.7.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.7.3" />
  </ItemGroup>

MSTest 4.2.3 is available. Let me upgrade to MSTest 4.x which definitely has `ThrowsExactly`. Actually, since the user asked for modern best practices, using MSTest 4.x would be ideal. But MSTest 4.x has breaking changes (like `ThrowsException` → `ThrowsExactly`). Let me just use the MSTest meta-package for simplicity, and upgrade to 4.x. 

Actually, with MSTest 4.x, you can use the `MSTest` meta-package instead of separate TestAdapter/TestFramework packages. Let me modernize the project file too.

data.content:
All 5 tests pass. Here's a summary of the modernizations applied:

| Anti-pattern | Fix |
|---|---|
| `public class` | `public sealed class` |
| Nullable `TestContext?` with `set` | `required TestContext` with `init` |
| `[TestInitialize]` for sync setup | Inline field initializer |
| `[ExpectedException]` | `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` |
| Vague test names (`Test1`) | Descriptive `Method_Scenario_Expected` names |
| Swapped `Assert.AreEqual` args | Correct order: `(expected, actual)` |
| Hard cast + `Assert.IsTrue` | `Assert.IsInstanceOfType<T>()` + `StringAssert.Contains` |
| Manual loop-based data-driven test | `[DataRow]` attributes |
| 3 separate MSTest packages | Single `MSTest` 4.2.3 meta-package |
